summary: Reducing K from 17 to 10 (Tier 2 step 5) gives a 28 percent Heron-depth reduction and 52x improvement in the quantum-vs-classical relative error.  The K=10 result (0.121) is within an order of magnitude of the classical tail (0.0095), while K=17 was 600x off.  This is the first compression strategy in this thread that actually moves the needle on QPU-vs-classical agreement.
takeaways: K=10 gives 28 percent Heron-depth reduction (1286 -> 919) and 39 percent 2Q-gate reduction (829 -> 509).
Relative error drops from 620x (K=17) to 12x (K=10), a 52x improvement.
The classical tail at K=10 is 0.95 percent (much higher than K=17's 0.03 percent because the sub-portfolio concentrates risk in 10 regions).
Mitigation still has small effect (Heron < 1 percent readout errors); the dominant error is still gate decoherence, but the circuit is now short enough that the QSVT signal can emerge.
Future Tier 2 work (QROM amplitude loading, QFT phase-kernel) would compound on this 28 percent reduction.
